mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, driving upward revisions to corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with positive RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC is above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and decelerating nonfarm payrolls signal labor market deterioration that precedes demand contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the central bank to maintain higher real interest rates for longer, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles, CPIAUCSL month-over-month acceleration does not predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC exceeding 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving supply-side momentum and manufacturing profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not positive in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized confirms aggregate demand strength, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations get revised upward.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth above 2.5% annualized does not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 30 basis point or greater rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market inflection that precedes consumer spending deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates concurrent with declining retail sales signal monetary policy overtightening into weakening demand, amplifying downside equity risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and falling RSAFS does not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
